clusters.title: 'I've never experienced something like that': Verstappen on crash in Australia qualifying – video
clusters.neutral_headline: Mercedes involved in crash in practice ahead of F1 qualifying
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC Sport

items.title: 'I've never experienced something like that': Verstappen on crash in Australia qualifying – video
items.standfirst: The four-time F1 world champion Max Verstappen will start the Australian Grand Prix from the back of the grid on Sunday after crashing in the first half of Q1. The Red Bull driver was unhurt from the crash. George Russell claimed pole while his Mercedes teammate Kimi Antonelli finished second.Russell on F1 Australian GP pole in Mercedes lockout as Verstappen crashes out Continue reading...
items.content: The four-time F1 world champion Max Verstappen will start the Australian Grand Prix from the back of the grid on Sunday after crashing in the first half of Q1. The Red Bull driver was unhurt from the crash. George Russell claimed pole while his Mercedes teammate Kimi Antonelli finished second.Russell on F1 Australian GP pole in Mercedes lockout as Verstappen crashes out Continue reading...

items.title: Mercedes involved in crash in practice ahead of F1 qualifying

items.content: George Russell will enter Saturday's qualifying session for the Australian Grand Prix having posted the fastest time in practice, while his teammate suffered a big crash late in the session.

items.title: Hundreds airlifted from remote Northern Territory communities amid major flooding
items.standfirst: Large-scale evacuation under way in Top End, while most of Queensland remains on flood watch Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA large-scale evacuation of hundreds of flood-affected Northern Territory residents is under way, as tropical lows bring heavy rain to large parts of the Top End and northern Queensland.Several flood warnings were in place for the NT on Saturday, including for the Katherine and Daly rivers, while most of Queensland remained on flood watch. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: The 20-year search for the Nannup Four
clusters.neutral_headline: The 20-year search for the Nannup Four

items.title: The 20-year search for the Nannup Four
items.standfirst: In July 2007, Simon Kadwill, Tony Popic, Chantelle McDougall and her five-year-old daughter Leela vanished from an isolated WA town. Chantelle's parents have spent the better part of 20 years trying to work out how to live with what's happened, as a complex picture of deceit, coercive control and cult-like beliefs has come to light.
items.content: In July 2007, Simon Kadwill, Tony Popic, Chantelle McDougall and her five-year-old daughter Leela vanished from an isolated WA town. Chantelle's parents have spent the better part of 20 years trying to work out how to live with what's happened, as a complex picture of deceit, coercive control and cult-like beliefs has come to light.
